text: This is the family line of Noah's sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, who had sons after the flood.
Japheth's sons were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
Gomer's sons were Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
Javan's sons were Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
From these the maritime peoples spread out into their lands, each with its own language, clan, and nation.
Ham's sons were Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
Cush's sons were Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca; Raamah's sons were Sheba and Dedan.
Cush fathered Nimrod, who became a mighty man on the earth.
He was a mighty hunter before the LORD, so it was said, 'like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD.'
His kingdom began with Babylon, Uruk, Akkad, and Kalneh, in the land of Shinar.
From that land he went to Assyria and built Nineveh, Rehoboth Ir, and Calah,
and Resen, between Nineveh and Calah — that great city.
Egypt fathered the Ludites, Anamites, Lehabites, Naphtuhites,
Pathrusites, Casluhites, and Caphtorites, from whom the Philistines came.
Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
and the Jebusites, Amorites, Girgashites,
Hivites, Arkites, Sinites,
Arvadites, Zemarites, and Hamathites; later the Canaanite clans scattered.
Canaan's borders stretched from Sidon toward Gerar, near Gaza, and toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboyim, near Lasha.
These are Ham's sons by clan, language, territory, and nation.
Shem, older brother of Japheth and ancestor of all the sons of Eber, also had sons.
Shem's sons were Elam, Ashur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.
Aram's sons were Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshek.
Arphaxad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.
Eber had two sons: Peleg, since in his days the earth was divided, and his brother Joktan.
Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab — all sons of Joktan.
Their territory stretched from Mesha toward Sephar, the eastern hill country.
These are Shem's sons by clan, language, territory, and nation.
These are the clans of Noah's sons by their family lines and nations, from whom the nations spread after the flood.
